name: defi-protocol-templates
description: Implement DeFi protocols with production-ready templates for staking, AMMs, governance, and lending systems. Use when building decentralized finance applications or smart contract protocols.
capabilities: Provides a staking rewards contract with reward distribution logic
Provides a simple AMM with liquidity provision and swap functionality
Provides governance token and proposal voting system templates
Provides a flash loan provider and receiver example
Includes documentation for five DeFi protocol patterns
author: wshobson
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only Solidity code examples in markdown. No executable scripts, network calls, file system access, or environment variable harvesting detected. All 74 static findings are false positives caused by the scanner misinterpreting Solidity code syntax and markdown formatting as security threats.
valueStatement: You need solid DeFi contract templates but do not want to start from scratch. This skill provides ready-to-use structures for staking, AMMs, governance, and lending protocols that you can adapt quickly.
limitations: No tests or deployment scripts are included
Templates are unaudited and need review before production use
No price oracle or liquidation logic is provided
Referenced external docs and assets are not in this package

useCases.title: Prototype a DeFi MVP
useCases.description: Adapt staking and AMM templates to validate a new protocol idea quickly.

useCases.title: Plan protocol scope
useCases.description: Review governance and flash loan examples to define product requirements.

useCases.title: Compare baseline patterns
useCases.description: Use templates as a baseline to spot missing controls in custom code.

promptTemplates.title: Staking basics
promptTemplates.prompt: Summarize the staking rewards template and list the key state variables and events.

promptTemplates.title: AMM setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Explain how addLiquidity and swap work in the AMM template and note any math assumptions.

promptTemplates.title: Governance flow
promptTemplates.prompt: Outline the governance proposal lifecycle in the template and list where custom logic should go.

promptTemplates.title: Flash loan safety
promptTemplates.prompt: Identify security checks in the flash loan template and suggest two additional safeguards.

bestPractices: Add comprehensive unit tests before deploying any adapted template
Use audited OpenZeppelin libraries for token and access control logic
Review math for overflow and rounding edge cases in production
antiPatterns: Deploying templates without a professional security audit
Skipping access control and pause mechanisms for critical functions
Ignoring token decimal handling and fee edge cases

faq.question: What platforms are compatible?
faq.answer: Templates are Solidity based and work on EVM chains that support OpenZeppelin libraries.

faq.question: What are the limits of these templates?
faq.answer: They are minimal examples without full oracle, liquidation, or upgrade proxy logic.

faq.question: How do I integrate this with my project?
faq.answer: Copy the contract and adapt interfaces, parameters, and access control for your needs.

faq.question: Does this skill access my data?
faq.answer: No. It contains static templates only with no data collection or external calls.

faq.question: What if compilation fails?
faq.answer: Check Solidity version compatibility and ensure OpenZeppelin dependencies are installed.

faq.question: How does this compare to full DeFi frameworks?
faq.answer: This is lighter and faster for prototyping but lacks complete production features.
